column2: Climatiq offers the world's most comprehensive emission factor database, aggregating over 80 datasets from 40+ sources, covering 300+ regions and 16+ economic sectors.
You can browse available emission factors and sources via our Data Explorer and review global coverage through our interactive map:

When searching in the Data Explorer you can filter on the left hand side based on your criteria, which will return a subset of relevant emission factors.
The search uses fuzzy matching, which means it matches not just exact terms, but also related terms or partial matches, helping you find relevant emission factors even if your input isn't exact.⁠

Below are examples of some search requests you could make, all demonstrating how the API deals with different types of input combinations.
You can use these inputs in the Excel Add-In, Postman Collection and in your own Python Scripts.
To see all the different search filtering possibilities, have a look at the "Request" table of parameters in our Search Documentation:
For the "Sector" and "Category" filters, please use the same options as displayed in the drop downs of our data explorer.

column13: When filtering for data within the Materials and Manufacturing sector, specifically for building materials and metals in Weight unit types, you will get a subset over 8000+ emission factors as of May 2025 from our Data Explorer.
When searching with this criteria, strictly a subset of emission factors within the "Energy" sector in Germany will be displayed.

The search query "consulting" of type "Money" will show only spend-based factors relating to the payment of consulting related activities. As of May 2025, Climatiq's data explorer has roughly 100 emission factors that meet this criteria.

The primary emission factor match returned by the Search feature for "cheese" is 3 results under the activity "consumer_goods-type_cheese" by EPA, applicable for the region US. There are 3 variations of the factor returned, each from the years 2022, 2023 and 2024. The Search endpoint will also specify the LCA stage (in this case, cradle to shelf), the raw emission factor, calculation methodology, constituent gas breakdown and other relevant information.
In the search request, 2 results were specified, so 2 emission factors were returned by EXIOBASE for aluminium in category Metals. One factor applies for the region code AT, and the other for AU.

In this search, 7 results were queried for polypropylene, with loose search filter criteria. 5/7 of the sources returned are by ecoinvent, and the other 2 are by OEKOBAUDAT with the LCA stage cradle to gate. You will also observe that the ecoinvent co2e total value will be empty or null, as access to ecoinvent raw emission factors is not possible. The unit for these factors are also Weight based in kg, with regions varying from DE, to the EU and even India (IN).

For 5 search results for cement in Germany, we get factors mostly from OEKOBAUDAT, since this is a German data source, and one from GEMIS. Multiple LCAs are covered, with options from cradle-to-gate, carbon_storage-cradle_to_gate and upstream-use_phase-transport. GEMIS provides the full constituent gas breakdown, while OEKOBAUDAT just provides the raw co2e value.

column2: For more extensive testing, you can find additional input data here. Of course you can also come up with your own inputs!
Searching via the Excel integration is also an easy and convenient way to get started with Climatiq's Excel add-in.
If you haven't already, you can follow the Excel set-up steps:
Below is an example of the results you can get from searching within Excel:

Depending on the search criteria, the API request will look different.
For searching, you will need to make a GET request using the url:

For example, if using terminal:
curl --request GET \
  --url 'https://api.climatiq.io/data/v1/search?query=cheese&data_version=^21&results_per_page=5&sector=consumer+goods+and+services&source=EPA&region=US' \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $CLIMATIQ_API_KEY" \
Alternatively, to define variables more clearly and avoid filtering using parameters in the URL string, download Insomnia REST API tester to easily send Search requests:
